meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on freelance projects. Use this intermediate SaaS starter kit guide featuring Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is 'boilerplate fatigue'—spending 80% of your time on auth, database schemas, and billing before writing a single feature. A battle-tested SaaS starter kit eliminates this friction, allowing you to bypass setup and ship functional, production-ready code in hours rather than weeks.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in an empty IDE. You have the vision, the client's deposit is in the bank, but the sheer gravity of setting up authentication, Stripe webhooks, and database migrations feels like wading through concrete. You find yourself stuck in a loop of configuration, losing momentum before the real work begins.

But what if the boring stuff didn't exist? By leveraging a high-leverage SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from manual labor to architectural orchestration. This isn't just about speed; it's about reclaiming your creative energy to focus on the features that actually drive revenue for your clients.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project with a production-grade foundation that includes pre-configured auth and database ORM.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, production-ready SaaS starter project using Next.js 14, Supabase for auth/DB, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure strict TypeScript types and modular directory structure.

steps.title: Configure Identity & Security
steps.description: Hardening your auth layer prevents the most common security bottlenecks in freelance delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Supabase Auth with Google OAuth and Email Magic Links. Configure Row Level Security (RLS) policies for a 'profiles' and 'projects' table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply RLS policies for multi-tenant isolation'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Billing
steps.description: Handling subscription logic is often where projects stall. Automate the webhook lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up Stripe Checkout and customer portal integration. Create a webhook handler for subscription updates that syncs to the Supabase 'subscriptions' table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add stripe webhook handler and sync subscription status'

steps.title: Build the Feature Scaffolding
steps.description: Use AI to generate boilerplate CRUD interfaces so you can focus on unique business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout with a sidebar and dynamic data table component that fetches from the 'projects' table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create dashboard UI with dynamic table component'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: A project isn't finished until it's live. Configure CI/CD to eliminate manual deployment friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and deploys to Vercel on push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate vercel deployment pipeline and github actions'

pro_tips: Always use a monorepo structure from day one; it makes scaling from a simple SaaS to a platform much easier for your clients later.
Never hardcode your env variables; use a validation library like T3 Env to ensure your app crashes on startup if a key is missing.
Implement 'Feature Flags' immediately, even for small projects, so you can test new code in production without breaking the client's current workflow.
Treat your starter kit like a product; version control your boilerplate so you can cherry-pick improvements from old projects into new ones.
Use AI-driven CLI tools to write your documentation; it keeps the project maintainable for the client long after you have moved on.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, audit your code for performance bottlenecks and prepare your documentation suite for client handoff.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished projects stall your freelance career. Build faster, ship more reliably, and elevate your reputation as a high-velocity developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
